title: Write 'Adding a Data Structure' contributor guide with 9-step walkthrough
description:
DX AUDIT: Every other contribution type has a guide (adding-an-algorithm.md rated 5/5, adding-a-module.md rated 5/5). The DS module — with the MOST complex registration process (9 locations vs 5 for algorithms) — has ZERO documentation. A developer literally cannot know where to start.

Write `docs/guides/adding-a-data-structure.md` covering:
1. Prerequisites
2. Create engine file (with template code block)
3. Register in index.ts (exports + DS_CATALOG entry)
4. ALL 9 DataStructuresModule.tsx locations with line numbers
5. Canvas visualization component guidelines
6. Test requirements (with test template)
7. Content quality checklist (step descriptions must explain WHY)
8. Common mistakes and how to avoid them
9. PR review checklist

Reference heap-ds.ts as gold standard engine, adding-an-algorithm.md as format reference.

Evidence: DX audit confirmed 0/5 documentation quality. Time savings: 60-120min → 5min for 'where to start'.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide exists at docs/guides/adding-a-data-structure.md
Covers all 9 mandatory change locations with file:line references
Includes copyable template code for engine file and test file
Includes quality checklist with minimum requirements
References heap-ds.ts as the gold standard implementation
CONTRIBUTING.md updated to mention DS contributions
A developer can follow guide to add a DS without asking questions

title: Add exhaustive never checks to all 3 critical switch statements
acceptanceCriteria: handleOperation switch has never exhaustiveness check
handleRandom switch has never exhaustiveness check
handleReset has never exhaustiveness check
DSCanvas if-chain converted to switch with never check
DSProperties switch has never exhaustiveness check
Adding a new DS to ActiveDS without handling it in ANY switch causes compile error

title: Extract shared step recorder utility — eliminate 33 copies of _stepId boilerplate
acceptanceCriteria: createStepRecorder() exported from types.ts
All 33 engine files use the shared utility
Zero module-level _stepId variables remain
Zero module-level resetStepId() functions remain
All existing tests pass

title: Add pre-commit hooks with lint-staged for format + typecheck
description:
DX AUDIT: No pre-commit hooks exist. Prettier has no config file (.prettierrc absent). A developer can commit unformatted code, and CI catches it late (after push, not before commit).

Add:
1. `.prettierrc` with explicit config (printWidth: 100, singleQuote: true, trailingComma: 'all')
2. `husky` + `lint-staged` pre-commit hook running: prettier --check, eslint --fix, tsc --noEmit on staged files
3. Add `format:check` to CI pipeline

This catches style and type issues BEFORE commit, saving CI round-trips.
acceptanceCriteria: .prettierrc exists with explicit configuration
husky pre-commit hook runs lint-staged
lint-staged runs prettier + eslint on staged .ts/.tsx files
Unformatted code cannot be committed
CI runs format:check as a quality gate

title: Remove 3 orphaned visualizer files that confuse contributors
description:
DX AUDIT: src/components/data-structures/ contains AVLTreeVisualizer.tsx, RedBlackTreeVisualizer.tsx, TreeCanvas.tsx — none are used by DataStructuresModule.tsx (which has inline AVLCanvas and RBTreeCanvas). A developer studying the codebase will spend time reading these files assuming they are the real implementation.

Either: (A) Delete them if truly unused, or (B) refactor DataStructuresModule to USE them instead of inline canvases.

Option A is recommended as the first step; option B is part of the monolith split (DST-069/070).

Evidence: DX audit codebase navigability section — 'orphaned components cause confusion'.
acceptanceCriteria: No orphaned visualizer files in src/components/data-structures/
Either deleted or actively used by DataStructuresModule
No references to deleted files remain in imports
TypeScript compiles cleanly

title: Add GitHub issue template for new data structure contributions
description:
DX AUDIT: GitHub issue templates exist for new_algorithm.yml and new_template.yml but NOT for new data structures. Contributors cannot file a structured request for a new DS.

Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/new_data_structure.yml with fields:
- DS name
- Category (linear/tree/hash/heap/probabilistic/system)
- Operations to support
- Complexity values
- Real-world applications
- Why this DS matters (motivation)
- References (CLRS chapter, Wikipedia link)
acceptanceCriteria: new_data_structure.yml exists in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/
Template has all required fields for a complete DS proposal
GitHub issue creation shows the DS template as an option
Template references the adding-a-data-structure.md guide
